qas.q: What instruction is the Advancement Manual for enlisted personnel of the U.S. Navy and the Navy Reserve?
qas.a: BUPERS INSTRUCTION 1430.16F

qas.q: What is the purpose of the advancement manual?
qas.a:
• Determining advancement eligibility requirements.
• Preparing necessary forms.
• Ordering, maintaining custody, and disposing of Navy-wide
advancement-in-rate examinations.
• Administering advancement-in-rate examinations.
• Changing rating.
• Responsibilities and procedures relating to advancement.

qas.q: Where can you find a definition for the terms used in the advancement manual?
qas.a: Terms can be found in Appendix A

qas.q: Which enlisted personnel does the manual apply to?
qas.a:
Personnel on the Regular Navy and Navy Reserve on Active Duty.
Personnel of Navy Reserve on Inactive Duty

qas.q: What personnel is considered active duty?
qas.a:
(1) Regular Navy and General Assignment Recall (USNR)
Active.
(2) Full Time Support (FTS).
(3) Canvasser Recruiter (CANREC) personnel recalled with obligated service for 1 year or more. Less than 31-day break between periods of active duty constitutes continuous active
service. Such personnel will participate in the active duty
examination process.
(4) Fleet Reserve and retired enlisted personnel recalled to active duty with the Regular Navy.
(5) Temporarily commissioned Limited Duty Officers (LDOs), U.S. Navy on active duty, whose permanent status is enlisted.

qas.q: Which Personnel of the Navy Reserve on Inactive duty is considered for advancement?
qas.a:
(1) Assigned to a Navy Reserve unit with a drilling requirement.
(2) Recalled for Active Duty for Special Work (ADSW)/1-Year Recall, recalled under Presidential Recall Authority or Mobilization regardless of the cumulative amount of time served on active duty.
(3) On Annual Training (AT)/Active Duty for Training (ADT)/Initial Active Duty for Training (IADT).

qas.q: What paygrade advancement regulations is covered by the advancement manual?
qas.a:
• Advancement to E2 and E3.
• Advancement-in-rate examinations.
• The Final Multiple Score (FMS). Refer to table 1-1 for FMS computation and table 1-2 for converting months to decimal values.
• Advancement to E7/8/9 by selection board action.
• Special selection boards.
• E6 examination qualification requirement for LDO.
• Other methods of advancement/change in rating of personnel
to include Combat Meritorious Advancement Program (CMAP), Posthumous Advancement, Recruiting Meritorious Advancement, and Command Advancement Program (CAP).

qas.q: What is the process for advancement from E2 through E3?
qas.a:
Automatic advancement for personnel meeting minimum time-in-rate (TIR) requirements.
No exam or evaluation required.

qas.q: What are the requirements for advancement to E4 through E7?
qas.a:
Examinations are required.
FMS system considers "whole person" i.e. experience, knowledge, performance.

qas.q: What comprises the FMS for E7?
qas.a: Examination score and Performance Evaluations.

qas.q: What comprises the FMS for E4-E6?
qas.a: Advancement-in-rate examination score, performance evaluations, service in paygrade, awards, previous examination performances.

qas.q: What is the maximum PMA points for an advancement candidate?
qas.a: Maximum PMA = 4.00

qas.q: What determines TIR for Reservists?
qas.a:
For Inactive Reserves, TIR is the same as SIPG, TIR = SIPG + DSPG.

PS: Find the definition for the terms: SIPG and DSPG.

qas.q: What does advancement from paygrades E7-E9 require?
qas.a:
Advancement to E7, E8, and E9 requires
selection board action.

qas.q: How are E7-E9 candidates qualified for selection board?
qas.a: E7 candidates must be designated selection board eligible (SBE) by competing in a Navy-wide advancement examination and meeting final multiple requirements for their rate.

qas.q: How are E8-E9 candidates designated SBE?
qas.a: E8 and E9 candidates are designated SBE on the basis of their CO/OIC recommendation and the TIR eligibility

qas.q: When are selection boards convened?
qas.a: Selection boards are convened annually by Chief of Naval Personnel (CHNAVPERS).

qas.q: Which personnel is eligible to special selection boards?
qas.a: Individuals who were not properly considered through the normal selection board process due to circumstances beyond their control.

qas.q: When are special selection boards held?
qas.a: Held annually, or as required, to consider any member who was eligible but not properly considered by the last regular board.

qas.q: Who do special selection boards candidates compete against?
qas.a: Those members will be compared to their competitive group from the last regular selection board.

qas.q: What authority determines eligibility for a special selection board?
qas.a:
CHNAVPERS
Sole authority for approving the report of a special enlisted selection board.

qas.q: When does the special selection board eligibility terminate?
qas.a: 9 months after the regular board convenes.

qas.q: Who is responsible to ensure the candidate's name appears in the selection board eligibility list?
qas.a: The member is equally responsible as his/her command to ensure name appears as special board eligible.

qas.q: What authority is to be contacted if member's name still does not appear after all efforts are exhausted?
qas.a: NAVPERSCOM (PERS 811/812)

qas.q: To which authority must the candidate submit their request for a special selection board?
qas.a: NAVPERSCOM (PERS 811/812)

qas.q: Which candidates must submit their special selection board advancement request?
qas.a: PERS 812

qas.q: What info must be included in this request?
qas.a:
1. Applicant's name, rate, last four of SSN,
PNEC.
2. Specific regular board that did not consider or improperly considered the applicant (e.g., FY-08 E8/9
Selection Board).
3. Summary of administrative error(s) that precluded the applicant's proper consideration and attempts made to correct the errors.
4. Miscellaneous issues (e.g., applicant is approaching high year tenure (HYT), etc.)
5. Command endorsement to include why member
will not have another opportunity to appear in front of the nextregular scheduled selection board (if applicable).
6. Any documentation supporting applicant's
request.

qas.q: Who makes the final eligibility decision on special selection board requests?
qas.a: CHNAVPERS

qas.q: Which records will the applicant's be compared to?
qas.a: Those in the previously held regular board.

qas.q: If selected, what will be the date of the candidate's advancements?
qas.a: The same date as the candidates who advanced in the previous board.

qas.q: What is the E6 requirement for recommendation for LDO?
qas.a: E6 personnel must pass the E7 advancement-in-rate examination prior to submitting a package for the LDO selection board.

qas.q: Which instruction provides info for enlisted personnel transition to officer status?
qas.a: OPNAVINST 1420.1A

qas.q: What are the CO responsibility in adherence to the advancement manual regulations?
qas.a:
(1) To properly administer advancement examinations to recommended, eligible, and fully qualified candidates.
(2) To properly maintain the security of examination
materials.
(3) To obtain and distribute the Bibliography for
Advancement-in-Rate Exam Study Guide (BIB). BIB's may be downloaded from https://www.advancement.cnet.navy.mil.
(4) To ensure dates of examinations, deadline for
completion of requirements, and the requirement for signing worksheets are disseminated throughout the command.
(5) To designate a local educational services officer
(ESO) in writing to act as liaison between the local ESO and ESO agreeing to administer an examination.
(6) To forward examinations for those candidates who will be on leave, Temporary Additional Duty (TEMADD), or in a transient status because of permanent changes of station (PCS)
on regularly scheduled examination date.

qas.q: What is NETPDTC's responsibilities in the NEAS?
qas.a:
(1) To develop, publish, and distribute Navy-wide
advancement-in-rate examinations for the purpose of testing and rank-ordering personnel who are qualified for advancement.
(2) To maintain accountability records of Navy-wide
advancement-in-rate examinations and process examination returns.
(3) To publish Navy-wide advancement-in-rate examination results.
(4) To act for CHNAVPERS as rate change authorization issuing authority for advancement to E4 through E9.
(5) To develop statistical data to maintain validity of
the examination system.
(6) To publish advancement examination BIBs.
(7) To facilitate Advancement Exam Development
Conferences (AEDC) utilizing rating Subject Matter Experts(SMEs) identified by Naval Personnel Development Command (NPDC) and Commander, U.S. Fleet Forces Command (COMUSFLTFORCOM).
(8) To publish a 2-year schedule broken into 8 quarters with approximately 12 AEDCs per quarter schedules.
(9) To publish a quarterly schedule with exact dates of upcoming AEDCs no later than 120 days prior to the first AEDC in that quarter.
(10) To develop, update, and provide a checklist
containing information required by NETPDTC to NPDC and COMUSFLTFORCOM for designated rating SMEs to complete.

qas.q: What are Naval Personnel Development Command (NPDC) Responsibilities?
qas.a:
(1) To coordinate with COMUSFLTFORCOM and applicable Type Commanders (TYCOMs) to designate the best-qualified rating SMEs (usually 6) covering a wide diversity of experience in the
rating for participation in the rating AEDCs.
(2) To coordinate with COMUSFLTFORCOM and applicable TYCOMs to designate the best-qualified rating SMEs to perform validation and verification of the rating advancement examinations per NETPDTC's examination development and
administration schedule (usually the end of March and the end of September).
(3) To keep NETPDTC informed of any major changes in the applicable rating and provide an SME to review the advancement exams when a fleet representative has not been identified.

qas.q: What action does NPDC have to take once SMEs are identified?
qas.a:
a)Complete the checklist provided by NETPDTC to ensure they meet all eligibility requirements for working with the rating advancement exams. This checklist will be provided to NETPDTC no later than 30 days prior to the start of the AEDC.
(b) Direct the SMEs to coordinate their travel with
SATO and provide an itinerary to NPDC.
(c) Forward the complete checklists and itineraries
to NETPDTC.

qas.q: Who announces SME nomination procedures?
qas.a: NETPDTC, in conjunction with NPDC and COMUSFLTFORCOM, will announce nomination procedures by message before each AEDC.

qas.q: What SME selection procedures should be used by major commands?
qas.a: Major commands will use a command nomination process. SME selection should be designed to select the most capable SMEs available.

qas.q: What is the criteria for selecting overseas SMEs?
qas.a: Do not select members from overseas units unless it is determined to be in the best interest of the rating's advancement exam.

qas.q: When should major commands send nominations be received by NETPDTC?
qas.a: No later than 45 days prior to the scheduled AEDC.

qas.q: What must the SME's command be notified about by a major command?
qas.a:
Advise the SME's command that NETPDTC will
provide accounting data and arrange lodging 2 weeks prior to AEDC commencement.

qas.q: What timelines must the major command comply with?
qas.a:
Comply with required timelines specified in the
AEDC message.

qas.q: What criteria must direct unit commanders consider when interviewing SME candidates?
qas.a:
1. Hold the rank of chief, senior chief or master chief. Frocked chiefs are discouraged.
2. Possess the skill level for and work in the associated rating.
3. Be familiar with the major types of equipment and duties in their specialty, have extensive background in the rating (including technical training where appropriate courses are available), and have working knowledge of primary reference material in the rating.
4. Have good communication skills, both in verbal and writing abilities.
5. Have no personal or future TEMADD requirements that would interfere with the availability for the entire AEDC TEMADD, excluding unforeseen emergencies.
6. Have no spouse or immediate family members in the Navy below the rank of chief in the same rating or family member married to someone below the rank of chief in the same rating.

qas.q: What are the responsibilities of the COMUSFLTFORCOM and TYCOM SME representatives?
qas.a:
(a)Participate in the associated rating's AEDC.
(b) developing a 2-year (for most ratings)
Topic/Sub-topic Subject/Sub-Subject Matrix for exam planning.
(c) reviewing content and clarity of previously used
multiple-choice administrative exam questions for re-use in later exams.
(d) writing new multiple-choice questions
appropriate to the skill and paygrade being tested using standard language and terminology. These questions will be tied to a reference and an Occupational/Naval Standard.
(e) tying exam questions to a reference and
SkillObject™ Level 1 Task or equivalent.
(f) assisting the NETPDTC assigned Rating Team
Leader in the development of the applicable series-rating exams.
Using NETPDTC exam writing guidelines, Subject/Sub-Subject Matrix and exam questions reviewed and/or written by the most recent AEDC.

qas.q: How many Chiefs participate in AEDCs?
qas.a: Normally 6 Chiefs

qas.q: Who reviews the rating's advancement exams?
qas.a:
An expert from the rating will review the applicable
associated series rating exams for content, clarity, flow,correctness and recommend changes to the assigned NETPDTC Team Leader.
